title: Authors and Citation
text: Hodge D (2026). ggscribe: Annotate 'ggplot2' Visualisation. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/davidhodge931/ggscribe.
code: @Manual{,   title = {ggscribe: Annotate 'ggplot2' Visualisation},   author = {David Hodge},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/davidhodge931/ggscribe}, }

title: Annotate ggplot2 Visualisation
text: objective ggscribe provide helpers annotate ‘ggplot2’ Visualisation

title: Annotate ggplot2 Visualisation
text: Install CRAN, development version GitHub.

title: Annotate ggplot2 Visualisation
code: library(ggplot2) #> Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.5.3 library(dplyr) #> Warning: package 'dplyr' was built under R version 4.5.3 #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union  set_theme(   ggrefine::theme_grey(     panel_heights = rep(unit(50, "mm"), 100),     panel_widths = rep(unit(75, "mm"), 100),   ) )  ggplot2::mpg |>   dplyr::mutate(drv = dplyr::case_when(     drv == "4" ~ "4-wheel",     drv == "f" ~ "Front",     drv == "r" ~ "Rear",   )   ) |>   ggplot(aes(x = displ, y = hwy, fill = drv, colour = drv)) +   geom_point() +   scale_fill_discrete(palette = jumble::jumble) +   scale_colour_discrete(palette = blends::multiply(jumble::jumble)) +   ggrefine::refine_modern() +   #required for ggscribe   coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +   #top labels of low, normal, high   ggscribe::annotate_axis_text(     y = c(20, 30, 40),     element_to = "blank",     tick_length = rel(-1),     hjust = 0,     vjust = -0.5,   ) +   ggscribe::annotate_axis_text(     position = "top",     x = I(0),     label = "Low",     tick_length = rel(0),     hjust = 0,   ) +   ggscribe::annotate_axis_text(     position = "top",     x = I(0.5),     label = "Normal",     tick_length = rel(0),     hjust = 0.5,   ) +   ggscribe::annotate_axis_text(     position = "top",     x = I(1),     label = "High",     tick_length = rel(0),     hjust = 1,   ) +   #hack to create space between legend and right labels   scale_y_continuous(     position = "right",     labels = \(x) paste0(x, rep("              ")),     name = NULL,   ) +   #inefficient shade and labels   ggscribe::annotate_panel_shade(ymax = 20, , fill = flexoki::flexoki$red["red200"]) +   ggscribe::annotate_axis_text(     position = "right",     y = c(20),   ) +   ggscribe::annotate_axis_ticks(     position = "right",     y = c(20),   ) +   ggscribe::annotate_axis_text(     position = "right",     y = mean(c(min(ggplot2::mpg$hwy), 20)),     label = "Inefficient",     element_to = "transparent",   ) +   #efficient shade and labels   ggscribe::annotate_panel_shade(ymin = 30, fill = flexoki::flexoki$green["green200"]) +   ggscribe::annotate_axis_text(     position = "right",     y = c(30),   ) +   ggscribe::annotate_axis_ticks(     position = "right",     y = c(30),   ) +   ggscribe::annotate_axis_text(     position = "right",     y = mean(c(30, max(ggplot2::mpg$hwy))),     label = "Efficient",   ) +   #titles   labs(     y = NULL,     title = "Highway fuel economy",     subtitle = "By displacement and drive train\n\n",   )

what: A mapped aesthetic for text colour on fill — aes_contrast
title: A mapped aesthetic for text colour on fill — aes_contrast
text: Modifies mapped colour (fill) aesthetic contrast fill (colour) aesthetic. Function can spliced ggplot2::aes rlang::!!!.

title: A mapped aesthetic for text colour on fill — aes_contrast
code: aes_contrast(..., dark = NULL, light = NULL, aesthetic = "colour")

title: A mapped aesthetic for text colour on fill — aes_contrast
text: ... Require named arguments (support trailing commas). dark dark colour. NULL, derived theme text panel background. light light colour. NULL, derived theme text panel background. aesthetic aesthetic modified contrast. Either "colour" (default) "fill".

title: A mapped aesthetic for text colour on fill — aes_contrast
text: ggplot2 aesthetic ggplot2::aes.

title: A mapped aesthetic for text colour on fill — aes_contrast
code: library(ggplot2) library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: ‘dplyr’ #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:stats’: #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:base’: #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union library(stringr)  mtcars |>   count(cyl, am) |>   mutate(     am = if_else(am == 0, "automatic", "manual"),     am = str_to_sentence(am),     cyl = as.factor(cyl)   ) |>   ggplot(aes(x = am, y = n, fill = cyl, label = n)) +   geom_col(     position = position_dodge(preserve = "single"),     width = 0.75,   ) +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("4" = "navy", "6" = "orange", "8" = "pink")) +   geom_text(     mapping = aes_contrast(),     position = position_dodge(width = 0.75, preserve = "single"),     vjust = 1.33,     show.legend = FALSE,   ) +   scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(c(0, 0.05)))   mtcars |>   count(cyl, am) |>   mutate(     am = if_else(am == 0, "automatic", "manual"),     am = stringr::str_to_sentence(am),     cyl = as.factor(cyl)   ) |>   ggplot(aes(y = am, x = n, fill = cyl, label = n)) +   geom_col(     position = position_dodge(preserve = "single"),     width = 0.75,   ) +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("4" = "navy", "6" = "orange", "8" = "pink")) +   geom_text(     mapping = aes_contrast(),     position = position_dodge(width = 0.75, preserve = "single"),     hjust = 1.25,     show.legend = FALSE,   ) +   scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(c(0, 0.05)))

title: Annotate an axis line — annotate_axis_line
text: Draws line style defaults taken axis.line element set theme. Typically used place line along axis edge interior reference line, optional xmin/xmax/ymin/ymax draw partial line. Lines along outside panel boundary requires coord_cartesian(clip = "").

title: Annotate an axis line — annotate_axis_line
code: annotate_axis_line(   ...,   position = NULL,   x = NULL,   y = NULL,   xmin = NULL,   xmax = NULL,   ymin = NULL,   ymax = NULL,   xend = NULL,   yend = NULL,   curvature = NULL,   angle = 90,   ncp = 5,   colour = NULL,   linewidth = NULL,   linetype = NULL,   element_to = "keep" )

title: Annotate an axis line — annotate_axis_line
text: ... used. Allows trailing commas named-argument style calls. position One "top", "bottom", "left", "right". Axis line mode . x axis line mode, single x value vertical line. segment/curve mode, x start position. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). y axis line mode, single y value horizontal line. segment/curve mode, y start position. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). xmin, xmax Start end x positions horizontal axis line. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). Axis line mode . ymin, ymax Start end y positions vertical axis line. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). Axis line mode . xend, yend End position segment curve. Providing x, y, xend, yend triggers segment/curve mode. curvature Amount curvature. Negative curves left, positive curves right, zero straight. NULL (default) draws straight segment. angle Skew angle curve control points (0-180). Used curvature non-NULL. Defaults 90. ncp Number curve control points. Higher values give smoother curves. Used curvature non-NULL. Defaults 5. colour Inherits axis.line set theme. linewidth Inherits axis.line set theme. Supports rel(). linetype Inherits axis.line set theme. element_to One "keep", "transparent", "blank". Controls whether native theme axis line suppressed. Defaults "keep". Axis line mode .

title: Annotate an axis line — annotate_axis_line
text: list ggplot2 annotation layers theme elements.

title: Annotate an axis line — annotate_axis_line
text: Can also draw straight segment curve two arbitrary points x, y, xend, yend provided.

title: Annotate an axis line — annotate_axis_line
code: library(ggplot2)  set_theme(theme_classic())  p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point() +   coord_cartesian(clip = "off")  # Replace the bottom axis line p + annotate_axis_line(position = "bottom", element_to = "transparent")   # Partial bottom axis between x = 2 and x = 4 p + annotate_axis_line(position = "bottom", xmin = 2, xmax = 4, element_to = "transparent")   # Vertical rule at x = 3.5 p + annotate_axis_line(x = 3.5)   # Straight line between two data points p + annotate_axis_line(x = 2, y = 15, xend = 5, yend = 30)   # Curved line between two data points p + annotate_axis_line(x = 2, y = 15, xend = 5, yend = 30, curvature = 0.3)

title: Annotate axis text — annotate_axis_text
text: Draws text labels specified break positions along axis, style defaults taken axis.text element set theme. Text along outside panel boundary requires coord_cartesian(clip = ""). Can also place labels arbitrary (x, y) coordinates x y provided.

title: Annotate axis text — annotate_axis_text
code: annotate_axis_text(   ...,   position = NULL,   x = NULL,   y = NULL,   label = NULL,   colour = NULL,   size = NULL,   family = NULL,   tick_length = NULL,   hjust = NULL,   vjust = NULL,   angle = 0,   element_to = "keep" )

title: Annotate axis text — annotate_axis_text
text: ... used. Allows trailing commas named-argument style calls. position One "top", "bottom", "left", "right". Inferred x/y provided. x vector x positions. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). combined y, triggers arbitrary positioning mode. y vector y positions. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). combined x, triggers arbitrary positioning mode. label vector labels function takes breaks returns labels. Defaults formatted break values. colour Inherits axis.text set theme. size Inherits axis.text set theme. family Inherits axis.text set theme. tick_length Offset axis edge grid unit, including tick length margin. Supports rel(). Negative values place labels inside panel. Axis mode . hjust, vjust Justification. Auto-calculated position NULL. angle Text rotation angle. Defaults 0. element_to One "keep", "transparent", "blank". Controls whether native theme axis text suppressed. Defaults "keep". Axis mode .

title: Annotate axis text — annotate_axis_text
text: list ggplot2 annotation layers theme elements.

title: Annotate axis text — annotate_axis_text
code: library(ggplot2)  set_theme(theme_classic())  p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point() +   coord_cartesian(clip = "off")  # Bottom axis labels at specific breaks p + annotate_axis_text(position = "bottom", x = c(2, 3, 4, 5))   # Custom labels p + annotate_axis_text(position = "bottom", x = c(2, 3, 4, 5),                        label = c("two", "three", "four", "five"))   # Inward labels using negative length p + annotate_axis_text(position = "bottom", x = c(2, 3, 4, 5),                        tick_length = grid::unit(-15, "pt"))   # Arbitrary positioning — label a specific point on the plot p + annotate_axis_text(x = 3.215, y = 21.4, label = "this one")

title: Annotate axis ticks — annotate_axis_ticks
text: Draws axis ticks specified break positions, style defaults taken axis.ticks element set theme. Ticks along outside panel boundary requires coord_cartesian(clip = "").

title: Annotate axis ticks — annotate_axis_ticks
code: annotate_axis_ticks(   ...,   position = NULL,   x = NULL,   y = NULL,   minor = FALSE,   colour = NULL,   linewidth = NULL,   tick_length = NULL,   element_to = "keep" )

title: Annotate axis ticks — annotate_axis_ticks
text: ... used. Allows trailing commas named-argument style calls. position One "top", "bottom", "left", "right". x vector x-axis break positions top/bottom ticks. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). y vector y-axis break positions left/right ticks. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). minor Logical. TRUE, uses minor tick theme defaults. Defaults FALSE. colour Inherits axis.ticks set theme. linewidth Inherits axis.ticks set theme. Supports rel(). tick_length Total tick length grid unit. Supports rel() scale relative theme default. Negative values flip tick direction. element_to One "keep", "transparent", "blank". Controls whether native theme ticks suppressed. Defaults "keep".

title: Annotate axis ticks — annotate_axis_ticks
text: list ggplot2 annotation layers theme elements.

title: Annotate axis ticks — annotate_axis_ticks
code: library(ggplot2)  set_theme(theme_classic())  p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point() +   coord_cartesian(clip = "off")  # Bottom ticks at specific breaks p + annotate_axis_ticks(position = "bottom", x = c(2, 3, 4, 5))   # Left ticks with native ticks suppressed p + annotate_axis_ticks(position = "left", y = c(10, 20, 30), element_to = "transparent")   # Inward ticks using a negative length p + annotate_axis_ticks(position = "bottom", x = c(2, 3, 4, 5), tick_length = grid::unit(-5, "pt"))   # Minor ticks p + annotate_axis_ticks(position = "bottom", x = seq(2, 5, by = 0.5), minor = TRUE)

title: Annotate panel grid lines — annotate_panel_grid
text: Draws grid lines specified break positions style defaults taken panel.grid element set theme. Specify x vertical lines y horizontal lines.

title: Annotate panel grid lines — annotate_panel_grid
code: annotate_panel_grid(   ...,   x = NULL,   y = NULL,   xmin = NULL,   xmax = NULL,   ymin = NULL,   ymax = NULL,   minor = FALSE,   colour = NULL,   linewidth = NULL,   linetype = NULL,   element_to = "keep" )

title: Annotate panel grid lines — annotate_panel_grid
text: ... used. Allows trailing commas named-argument style calls. x vector x-axis breaks vertical grid lines. used together y. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). y vector y-axis breaks horizontal grid lines. used together x. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). xmin, xmax Start end x positions horizontal grid lines. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). Defaults -Inf Inf. ymin, ymax Start end y positions vertical grid lines. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). Defaults -Inf Inf. minor Logical. TRUE, uses minor grid theme defaults. Defaults FALSE. colour Inherits panel.grid.major panel.grid.minor set theme. linewidth Inherits panel.grid.major panel.grid.minor set theme. Supports rel(). linetype Inherits panel.grid.major panel.grid.minor set theme. element_to One "keep", "transparent", "blank". Controls whether native theme grid lines suppressed. Defaults "keep".

title: Annotate panel grid lines — annotate_panel_grid
text: list ggplot2 annotation layers theme elements.

title: Annotate panel grid lines — annotate_panel_grid
code: library(ggplot2)  set_theme(theme_minimal())  p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point()  # Vertical grid lines at specific x breaks p + annotate_panel_grid(x = c(2, 3, 4, 5))   # Horizontal grid lines at specific y breaks, native lines suppressed p + annotate_panel_grid(y = c(10, 20, 30), element_to = "transparent")   # Minor vertical grid lines p + annotate_panel_grid(x = seq(2, 5, by = 0.5), minor = TRUE)   # Partial horizontal lines that don't span the full panel width p + annotate_panel_grid(y = c(15, 25), xmax = I(0.5), element_to = "transparent")

what: Annotate a shaded panel region — annotate_panel_shade
title: Annotate a shaded panel region — annotate_panel_shade
text: Draws filled rectangle panel colour defaults taken set theme. Defaults subtle overlay across full panel, fill automatically adapting light dark panel backgrounds.

title: Annotate a shaded panel region — annotate_panel_shade
code: annotate_panel_shade(   ...,   xmin = -Inf,   xmax = Inf,   ymin = -Inf,   ymax = Inf,   fill = "#878580",   alpha = 0.25,   colour = "transparent",   linewidth = NULL,   linetype = NULL )

title: Annotate a shaded panel region — annotate_panel_shade
text: ... used. Allows trailing commas named-argument style calls. xmin, xmax Left right edges rectangle. Defaults -Inf Inf. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). ymin, ymax Bottom top edges rectangle. Defaults -Inf Inf. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). fill Fill colour. Defaults neutral grey. alpha Opacity rectangle. Defaults 0.25. colour Border colour. Defaults "transparent". linewidth Inherits panel.border set theme. Supports rel(). linetype Border linetype. Defaults 1.

title: Annotate a shaded panel region — annotate_panel_shade
text: list containing annotation layer.

title: Annotate a shaded panel region — annotate_panel_shade
code: library(ggplot2)  set_theme(theme_classic())  p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point()  # Shade the full panel p + annotate_panel_shade()   # Shade a specific data range p + annotate_panel_shade(xmin = 3, xmax = 4)   # Shade using normalized coordinates p + annotate_panel_shade(xmin = I(0.25), xmax = I(0.75))   # Custom fill and opacity p + annotate_panel_shade(ymin = 20, ymax = 30, fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.15)

title: ggscribe: Annotate 'ggplot2' Visualisation — ggscribe-package
text: Annotation helpers 'ggplot2' visualisations, style defaults inherited active theme. includes annotation functions cover axis lines, ticks, text, panel grid lines, shaded regions. makes straightforward annotate plots way stays consistent set theme.

title: ggscribe: Annotate 'ggplot2' Visualisation — ggscribe-package
text: Maintainer: David Hodge davidhodge931@gmail.com (ORCID) [copyright holder]
